proposed_from.topic: Partial identification of average direct treatment effects under partially-misspecified network exposure mappings, when interference structure is only locally known
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"Closest in-catalogue material remains the PartialID seed catalogue and the pid_dynamic_iv_compliance_v2 proposal on assignment-memory mixture splitting for dynamic IV bounds; the gap is that those kernels concern latent compliance histories, not shared hidden-edge graph completions or exposure-event compatibility for network ADE bounds. No in-catalogue entry records a network-exposure partial-ID proposal with a shared graph-completion constraint.","published_axis":"The closest published causal comparators are SchroderOprescuFeuerriegelKallus2026 on sharp exposure-mapping misspecification bounds under known-network propensity-ratio sensitivity, WeinsteinNevo2026 on bias and robust estimation under candidate network misspecification, ZhangMohanPearl2023 on linear model uncertainty under interference, and Egami2020 on unobserved-network spillover sensitivity. The gap preserved in v4 is the shared-completion phenomenon: the proposal asks whether a cellwise ADE endpoint is unrealizable because positive and zero hidden-exposure demands cannot be induced by one locally feasible graph. Edmonds1965 and Wolsey1975 are now acknowledged as classical prior art for the certificate mechanics, with the causal novelty isolated to the signed-demand reduction and bound interpretation."}
seed_list: Prior work SchroderOprescuFeuerriegelKallus2026 would imply that sharp exposure-mapping sensitivity can be organized through unitwise exposure-propensity bounds. Our seed conjectures that those bounds are strictly nonsharp under locally known networks because one hidden edge must jointly determine two endpoint exposure errors.

Prior work WeinsteinNevo2026 would predict that robustness to network misspecification is captured by considering multiple candidate networks. Our seed conjectures that a local graph-completion polytope excludes extrema admitted by a multi-network or unitwise relaxation because candidate hidden edges must be mutually compatible.

Prior work Savje2024 would imply that misspecified exposure effects can remain precisely estimable once exposure definitions are separated from structural assumptions. Our seed conjectures that local direct effects become partially identified but not cellwise independent because local graph knowledge supplies cross-unit restrictions.

Prior work Leung2022 would predict that far-neighbor misspecification is mainly a decaying-bias or asymptotic-negligibility issue. Our seed conjectures that finite ADE bounds can have a degree-cap phase transition at the first hidden edge because edge sharing creates a discrete compatibility constraint.

Prior work GaoHarshawSavjeWang2026 would imply that broad exposure-map misspecification cannot be uniformly tested. Our seed conjectures that non-testability does not force vacuous bounds because local graph-completion cuts can still rule out cellwise sensitivity endpoints.

Prior work YuAiroldiBorgsChayes2022 would imply that unknown networks can be bypassed for total treatment effects under additive restrictions. Our seed conjectures that the same hidden networks can preserve total-effect information while flipping a local average direct effect through unobserved exposure states.

Prior work BelloniFangVolfovsky2022 would predict that searching over local radii can adapt to unknown interference patterns. Our seed conjectures that radius-adaptive local patterns can still leave a sharp partial-ID gap because radius choices for adjacent units must be realized by one graph.

Prior work AronowSamii2017 establishes design-based identification with a known exposure mapping. Our seed conjectures that when one hidden edge generates exposure misspecification, exposure probabilities cannot be perturbed independently across units because the design matrix inherits edge-incidence constraints.

Prior work ForastiereAiroldiMealli2021 would imply that generalized propensity scores balance local network exposures for direct-effect estimation. Our seed conjectures that balanced local GPS cells can still admit incompatible hidden completions, so the direct-effect bound is governed by graph feasibility rather than balancing alone.

Prior work HudgensHalloran2008 establishes direct/spillover decompositions under partial interference. Our seed conjectures that locally known cross-cluster hidden ties can preserve the decomposition notation while making the direct-effect identified set depend on shared-edge compatibility.

Prior work SavjeAronowHudgens2021 would imply that marginalized EATEs can be stable under unknown interference. Our seed conjectures that EATE stability can coexist with sign ambiguity for local ADE because hidden exposure states cancel in the marginal target but not in the direct contrast.

Prior work Manski1990 would predict bounded-outcome uncertainty gives worst-case intervals absent additional outcome restrictions. Our seed conjectures that graph-completion feasibility alone can tighten those intervals because some missing-potential-outcome assignments require impossible hidden exposure labels.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work SchroderOprescuFeuerriegelKallus2026 would imply that sharp exposure-mapping sensitivity can be organized through unitwise exposure-propensity bounds. Our seed conjectures that those bounds are strictly nonsharp under locally known networks because one hidden edge must jointly determine two endpoint exposure errors.

anchor_paper.author: Schroder, Oprescu, Feuerriegel, and Kallus
anchor_paper.claim: Derives sharp upper and lower bounds on direct and spillover effects under misspecified exposure mappings using exposure-propensity sensitivity bounds.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Not blocked: this is not merely weakening known-network assumptions; the claimed mechanism is a strict nonsharpness gap from shared hidden-edge compatibility absent in unitwise bounds.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from SchroderOprescuFeuerriegelKallus2026 because their bounds are already sharp under misspecification.
seed_details.rebuttal: Their sharpness is for the sensitivity model they define; the proposed model adds a different joint graph-completion restriction that can invalidate product-form unitwise extrema.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work WeinsteinNevo2026 would predict that robustness to network misspecification is captured by considering multiple candidate networks. Our seed conjectures that a local graph-completion polytope excludes extrema admitted by a multi-network or unitwise relaxation because candidate hidden edges must be mutually compatible.

anchor_paper.author: Weinstein and Nevo
anchor_paper.claim: Bounds bias from misspecified networks and proposes a multi-network estimator unbiased if at least one candidate network is correct.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Not blocked: the seed does not extend the estimator; it predicts a different sharp identified set caused by compatibility among hidden candidate edges.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from WeinsteinNevo2026 because they already handle multiple possible networks.
seed_details.rebuttal: They analyze bias and robust estimation, not the sharp partial-ID projection over a local graph-completion class for ADE.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work Savje2024 would imply that misspecified exposure effects can remain precisely estimable once exposure definitions are separated from structural assumptions. Our seed conjectures that local direct effects become partially identified but not cellwise independent because local graph knowledge supplies cross-unit restrictions.

anchor_paper.author: Savje
anchor_paper.claim: Separates exposure mappings as estimand definitions from exposure mappings as structural restrictions and gives conditions for inference under misspecification.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Not blocked: not a generic assumption weakening; the surprise is cross-unit graph compatibility inside a partial-ID target.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from Savje2024 because he already separates definitions from assumptions.
seed_details.rebuttal: Savje2024 motivates alternative restrictions but does not derive sharp finite graph-completion bounds or shared-edge nonsharpness of cellwise relaxations.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work Leung2022 would predict that far-neighbor misspecification is mainly a decaying-bias or asymptotic-negligibility issue. Our seed conjectures that finite ADE bounds can have a degree-cap phase transition at the first hidden edge because edge sharing creates a discrete compatibility constraint.

anchor_paper.author: Leung
anchor_paper.claim: Under approximate neighborhood interference, distant treatments can have smaller nonzero effects while standard IPW estimators remain consistent and asymptotically normal.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Potentially resembles radius extension, but not blocked because the proposed phenomenon is a finite sharp-bound phase transition at hidden degree cap K=1.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from approximate neighborhood interference because hidden far-neighbor effects are already allowed.
seed_details.rebuttal: ANI is an asymptotic estimation condition; it does not characterize finite sharp bounds or edge-sharing compatibility facets.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work GaoHarshawSavjeWang2026 would imply that broad exposure-map misspecification cannot be uniformly tested. Our seed conjectures that non-testability does not force vacuous bounds because local graph-completion cuts can still rule out cellwise sensitivity endpoints.

anchor_paper.author: Gao, Harshaw, Savje, and Wang
anchor_paper.claim: Any broad specification test for exposure mapping models has worst-case Type I and Type II errors summing to one without additional restrictions.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Not blocked: orthogonal to the blocklist; it turns a negative testing result into a positive sharp-bound compatibility question.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from the impossibility theorem because no data can distinguish the exposure models.
seed_details.rebuttal: The conjecture is conditional on explicit local graph-completion restrictions, not a uniform test over unrestricted alternatives.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work YuAiroldiBorgsChayes2022 would imply that unknown networks can be bypassed for total treatment effects under additive restrictions. Our seed conjectures that the same hidden networks can preserve total-effect information while flipping a local average direct effect through unobserved exposure states.

anchor_paper.author: Yu, Airoldi, Borgs, and Chayes
anchor_paper.claim: Shows total treatment effects can be estimated in randomized experiments with unknown network structure under additive network-effect restrictions.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Target variation from TTE to ADE, but not blocked because the new target reveals an identification gap hidden by the global total-effect formulation.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just the known difference between total and direct effects under interference.
seed_details.rebuttal: The proposed kernel is a sharp graph-completion sign/interval separation, not the definitional distinction between targets.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work BelloniFangVolfovsky2022 would predict that searching over local radii can adapt to unknown interference patterns. Our seed conjectures that radius-adaptive local patterns can still leave a sharp partial-ID gap because radius choices for adjacent units must be realized by one graph.

anchor_paper.author: Belloni, Fang, and Volfovsky
anchor_paper.claim: Studies neighborhood-adaptive estimators when interference radius and intensity are unknown on a known network.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Could look like extending adaptive radius to partial ID, but not blocked because the mechanism is failure of independent local pattern choices to paste into one graph.
seed_details.referee_objection: This follows from BelloniFangVolfovsky2022 because their procedure searches over local patterns.
seed_details.rebuttal: Their oracle inequalities concern estimation over known-network patterns; they do not impose or characterize graph-completion feasibility for hidden edges.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work AronowSamii2017 establishes design-based identification with a known exposure mapping. Our seed conjectures that when one hidden edge generates exposure misspecification, exposure probabilities cannot be perturbed independently across units because the design matrix inherits edge-incidence constraints.

anchor_paper.author: Aronow and Samii
anchor_paper.claim: Defines exposure mappings and identifies average potential outcomes under a known randomized design and known exposure mapping.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Not blocked: this is a failure mode of independent exposure-probability perturbations, not an extension of a known-exposure estimator.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from standard exposure-probability calculations once the graph is unknown.
seed_details.rebuttal: The standard calculation is per known graph; the new claim is a strict gap between joint graph-completion projections and product relaxations.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work ForastiereAiroldiMealli2021 would imply that generalized propensity scores balance local network exposures for direct-effect estimation. Our seed conjectures that balanced local GPS cells can still admit incompatible hidden completions, so the direct-effect bound is governed by graph feasibility rather than balancing alone.

anchor_paper.author: Forastiere, Airoldi, and Mealli
anchor_paper.claim: Identifies and estimates direct and interference effects in observational network studies using generalized propensity scores for own treatment and neighborhood exposure.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Not blocked: not an observational GPS extension; it highlights graph-completion incompatibility that remains after local exposure balancing.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from unmeasured confounding or misspecified GPS concerns.
seed_details.rebuttal: The proposed setting can be randomized; the ambiguity arises from hidden exposure labels, not ordinary covariate confounding.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work HudgensHalloran2008 establishes direct/spillover decompositions under partial interference. Our seed conjectures that locally known cross-cluster hidden ties can preserve the decomposition notation while making the direct-effect identified set depend on shared-edge compatibility.

anchor_paper.author: Hudgens and Halloran
anchor_paper.claim: Defines direct, indirect, total, and overall effects under interference and studies relations among them.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Could resemble generalizing partial interference, but not blocked because the proposed mechanism is cross-cluster hidden-edge compatibility in sharp bounds.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is already known because partial interference fails when cross-cluster ties exist.
seed_details.rebuttal: Failure of partial interference is known; the proposed contribution is a finite sharp identified set under locally bounded violations.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work SavjeAronowHudgens2021 would imply that marginalized EATEs can be stable under unknown interference. Our seed conjectures that EATE stability can coexist with sign ambiguity for local ADE because hidden exposure states cancel in the marginal target but not in the direct contrast.

anchor_paper.author: Savje, Aronow, and Hudgens
anchor_paper.claim: Shows common estimators can consistently estimate an expected average treatment effect under unknown limited interference.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Target variation from EATE to ADE, but not blocked because the seed asserts a sign-ambiguity separation hidden by marginalization.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just choosing a harder estimand than EATE.
seed_details.rebuttal: The proposed theorem would identify exactly which hidden graph completions preserve EATE while changing ADE bounds.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work Manski1990 would predict bounded-outcome uncertainty gives worst-case intervals absent additional outcome restrictions. Our seed conjectures that graph-completion feasibility alone can tighten those intervals because some missing-potential-outcome assignments require impossible hidden exposure labels.

anchor_paper.author: Manski
anchor_paper.claim: Provides nonparametric bounded-outcome treatment-effect bounds when counterfactual outcomes are unobserved.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Not blocked: orthogonal to listed transformation failures; it adds no outcome shape restriction but uses exposure feasibility as the tightening mechanism.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from adding any restriction to Manski bounds.
seed_details.rebuttal: The non-obvious part is that graph feasibility, not outcome monotonicity or covariates, supplies cross-unit compatibility inequalities for network ADE.

proposed_from.literature_map: Manski1990 (AER, Manski1990) gives bounded-outcome partial-ID logic; tension: network exposure feasibility can tighten bounds without outcome shape restrictions. HudgensHalloran2008 (JASA, HudgensHalloran2008) defines direct/indirect effects under interference; gap: local graph uncertainty is not a sharp-bounds object. AronowSamii2017 (AOAS, AronowSamii2017, doi:10.1214/16-AOAS1005) identifies exposure-specific means under known exposure mappings; tension: hidden edges make exposure probabilities jointly constrained. ForastiereAiroldiMealli2021 (JASA, ForastiereAiroldiMealli2021) uses generalized propensity scores for direct and interference effects on networks; gap: assumes the exposure structure used for balancing is correct enough. SavjeAronowHudgens2021 (Annals of Statistics, SavjeAronowHudgens2021, doi:10.1214/20-AOS1973) shows marginalized EATE can be estimated under unknown interference; tension: local direct effects may remain partially identified. Leung2022 (Econometrica, Leung2022, doi:10.3982/ECTA17841) develops approximate neighborhood interference; gap: asymptotic robustness rather than finite sharp graph-completion bounds. Savje2024 (Biometrika, Savje2024, doi:10.1093/biomet/asad019) separates exposure definitions from structural restrictions; open issue: what finite restrictions replace correct exposure maps. SchroderOprescuFeuerriegelKallus2026 (arXiv:2602.03459, SchroderOprescuFeuerriegelKallus2026) gives sharp exposure-propensity sensitivity bounds under misspecified mappings; tension: fully known network and unitwise ratio bounds do not encode shared hidden edges. WeinsteinNevo2026 (Biometrics, WeinsteinNevo2026, doi:10.1093/biomtc/ujag023) studies misspecified network structure and robust multi-network estimation; gap: not sharp partial-ID ADE over local completions. YuAiroldiBorgsChayes2022 (PNAS, YuAiroldiBorgsChayes2022, doi:10.1073/pnas.2208975119) handles total treatment effects with unknown networks; tension: global estimability can hide local direct-effect ambiguity. BelloniFangVolfovsky2022 (arXiv:2212.03683, BelloniFangVolfovsky2022) adaptively estimates unknown interference radius on a known graph; gap: estimation, not finite graph-completion sharpness. GaoHarshawSavjeWang2026 (arXiv:2605.09726, GaoHarshawSavjeWang2026) proves broad exposure-map specification tests are impossible; tension: partial-ID bounds under explicit local restrictions are still informative. Reused adjacent partial-ID context from pid_dynamic_iv_compliance_v2: Balke-Pearl/Manski dynamic-IV sharpness literature is trusted prior-art substrate but not the kernel here.
